name: CAMS_SSF5_OutcomeDisposition
title: CAMS SSF-5: Outcome / Disposition (Final Session)
publisher: SPiER Project (FHIR translation of the CAMS Suicide Status Form, 5th ed.)
description: FHIR Questionnaire for the CAMS SSF-5 Outcome/Disposition final session. The final CAMS session re-rates the six SSF Core Assessment vitals, confirms whether CAMS resolution criteria are met (three consecutive sessions of overall risk ≤ 2 with the patient managing suicidal thoughts/feelings and behaviorally stable), captures what made the difference and what to do differently if suicidal again, and records the episode disposition / next step. One of the CAMS SSF-5 session forms — all map to the single catalogued CAMS SSF-5 tool (TL-020).
purpose: Close the CAMS episode: document the final SSF vitals, the resolution determination, and the disposition (continue CAMS, resolved, refer to adjunctive treatment, or step up to a higher level of care).
copyright: The Suicide Status Form (SSF) and the CAMS framework are © David A. Jobes / CAMS-care, LLC, and are distributed via Guilford Press (Managing Suicidal Risk, 3rd ed.). Used by SPiER under the license/agreement held by the SPiER project; the CAMS SSF is a commercial instrument — do not reproduce without the CAMS-care/Guilford agreement.

item.text: CAMS final session. Re-rate the SSF Core Assessment, determine whether resolution criteria are met, and record the disposition.
Overall Risk of Suicide (1 = extremely low, 5 = extremely high)
Resolution criteria met? (three consecutive sessions of overall risk ≤ 2, managing thoughts/feelings, and behaviorally stable)
What made the difference (patient's own words)?
What will you do differently if you become suicidal again?

valueCoding.display: Refer to other / adjunctive treatment (e.g., group therapy)
Step up to a higher level of care (e.g., inpatient)
